sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Good morning! May I see your passport and ticket, please?
sentences.text_zh: 早上好！请出示您的护照和机票好吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˈmɔːrnɪŋ! meɪ aɪ siː jʊər ˈpɑːspɔːrt ənd ˈtɪkɪt, pliːz?/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Here you are.
sentences.text_zh: 给您。
sentences.phonetic: /hɪər juː ɑːr/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Thank you. Are you checking any baggage today?
sentences.text_zh: 谢谢。您今天有行李需要托运吗？
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː. ɑːr juː ˈtʃekɪŋ ˈeni ˈbæɡɪdʒ təˈdeɪ?/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Yes, I have one suitcase to check in.
sentences.text_zh: 是的，我有一个行李箱要托运。
sentences.phonetic: /jes, aɪ hæv wʌn ˈsuːtkeɪs tuː tʃek ɪn/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Please place it on the scale. Would you prefer a window or aisle seat?
sentences.text_zh: 请把它放在秤上。您想要靠窗还是靠走廊的座位？
sentences.phonetic: /pliːz pleɪs ɪt ɒn ðə skeɪl. wʊd juː prɪˈfɜːr ə ˈwɪndəʊ ɔːr aɪl siːt?/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Window seat, please.
sentences.text_zh: 靠窗座位，谢谢。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈwɪndəʊ siːt, pliːz/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Here's your boarding pass. Boarding starts at 10:30 AM at gate 23. Have a pleasant flight!
sentences.text_zh: 这是您的登机牌。10点30分开始在23号登机口登机。祝您旅途愉快！
sentences.phonetic: /hɪərz jʊər ˈbɔːrdɪŋ pɑːs. ˈbɔːrdɪŋ stɑːrts ət ten ˈθɜːrti eɪ em ət ɡeɪt ˈtwenti θriː. hæv ə ˈplezənt flaɪt!/

cultural_notes: In Western airports, passengers are expected to arrive 2-3 hours before international flights. Always have documents ready before approaching the counter.
practice_tips: Practice the phrase 'Here you are' - it's more natural than 'Here it is' when handing documents to someone.

sentences.speaker: Security
sentences.text_en: Please remove your laptop and liquids from your bag.
sentences.text_zh: 请从包里拿出您的笔记本电脑和液体物品。
sentences.phonetic: /pliːz rɪˈmuːv jʊər ˈlæptɒp ənd ˈlɪkwɪdz frɒm jʊər bæɡ/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Do I need to take off my shoes?
sentences.text_zh: 我需要脱鞋吗？
sentences.phonetic: /duː aɪ niːd tuː teɪk ɒf maɪ ʃuːz?/

sentences.speaker: Security
sentences.text_en: Yes, please. Also remove your belt and empty your pockets.
sentences.text_zh: 是的，请脱下。还请摘下您的皮带并掏空口袋。
sentences.phonetic: /jes, pliːz. ˈɔːlsəʊ rɪˈmuːv jʊər belt ənd ˈempti jʊər ˈpɒkɪts/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Okay, all done.
sentences.text_zh: 好的，都弄好了。
sentences.phonetic: /əʊˈkeɪ, ɔːl dʌn/

sentences.speaker: Security
sentences.text_en: Please step through the metal detector.
sentences.text_zh: 请通过金属探测器。
sentences.phonetic: /pliːz step θruː ðə ˈmetl dɪˈtektər/

sentences.speaker: Security
sentences.text_en: Thank you. You can collect your belongings now.
sentences.text_zh: 谢谢。您现在可以取回您的物品了。
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː. juː kæn kəˈlekt jʊər bɪˈlɒŋɪŋz naʊ/

cultural_notes: TSA (US) and similar security agencies have strict liquid rules: containers must be 100ml or less, placed in a clear plastic bag. Follow instructions promptly to avoid delays.
practice_tips: Practice the phrasal verb 'take off' (remove) vs 'take off' (plane departure) - same words, different meanings based on context.

sentences.speaker: Announcement
sentences.text_en: Attention passengers. We are now boarding rows 20 and above.
sentences.text_zh: 旅客请注意。我们现在开始登机，20排及以后的座位。
sentences.phonetic: /əˈtenʃən ˈpæsɪndʒərz. wiː ɑːr naʊ ˈbɔːrdɪŋ rəʊz ˈtwenti ənd əˈbʌv/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Excuse me, I'm in row 18. Should I wait?
sentences.text_zh: 打扰一下，我是18排的。我应该等一下吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɪkˈskjuːz miː, aɪm ɪn rəʊ ˈeɪˈtiːn. ʃʊd aɪ weɪt?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Yes, please wait for your row to be called. We'll board you shortly.
sentences.text_zh: 是的，请等待您的排数被叫到。我们很快会让您登机。
sentences.phonetic: /jes, pliːz weɪt fɔːr jʊər rəʊ tuː biː kɔːld. wiːl bɔːrd juː ˈʃɔːrtli/

sentences.speaker: Announcement
sentences.text_en: We are now inviting rows 10 through 19 to board.
sentences.text_zh: 我们现在邀请10排到19排的乘客登机。
sentences.phonetic: /wiː ɑːr naʊ ɪnˈvaɪtɪŋ rəʊz ten θruː ˈnaɪnˈtiːn tuː bɔːrd/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: May I see your boarding pass, please? You're in seat 18C.
sentences.text_zh: 请出示您的登机牌好吗？您是18C座位。
sentences.phonetic: /meɪ aɪ siː jʊər ˈbɔːrdɪŋ pɑːs, pliːz? jʊər ɪn siːt ˈeɪˈtiːn siː/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Thank you. Which way should I go?
sentences.text_zh: 谢谢。我应该往哪边走？
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː. wɪtʃ weɪ ʃʊd aɪ ɡəʊ?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Turn right and proceed to the rear of the aircraft. Have a nice flight!
sentences.text_zh: 右转然后往飞机后部走。祝您旅途愉快！
sentences.phonetic: /tɜːn raɪt ənd prəˈsiːd tuː ðə rɪər ɒv ði ˈeəkrɑːft. hæv ə naɪs flaɪt!/

cultural_notes: Airlines board by zones or rows to manage flow. Priority boarding often includes first class, business class, families with children, and loyalty program members.
practice_tips: Listen for key boarding announcements: 'rows', 'zones', 'now boarding'. Seat designations follow pattern: Row Number + Letter (e.g., 18C).

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Good evening. I have a reservation under the name Smith.
sentences.text_zh: 晚上好。我有一个预订，名字是Smith。
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˈiːvnɪŋ. aɪ hæv ə ˌrezəˈveɪʃən ˈʌndər ðə neɪm smɪθ/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Welcome, Mr. Smith. Let me pull up your reservation. I see you booked a deluxe room for three nights?
sentences.text_zh: 欢迎，Smith先生。让我调出您的预订。我看到您预订了豪华房三晚？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈwelkəm, ˈmɪstər smɪθ. let miː pʊl ʌp jʊər ˌrezəˈveɪʃən. aɪ siː juː bʊkt ə dɪˈlʌks ruːm fɔːr θriː naɪts?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: That's correct. Does the room have a city view?
sentences.text_zh: 没错。房间有城市景观吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ðæts kəˈrekt. dʌz ðə ruːm hæv ə ˈsɪti vjuː?/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Yes, you'll be in room 1508 on the 15th floor with a wonderful city view. May I have your ID and credit card, please?
sentences.text_zh: 是的，您将入住15楼的1508房间，有很棒的城市景观。请出示您的身份证件和信用卡好吗？
sentences.phonetic: /jes, juːl biː ɪn ruːm ˈfɪfˈtiːn əʊ eɪt ɒn ðə ˈfɪfˈtiːnθ flɔːr wɪð ə ˈwʌndərfəl ˈsɪti vjuː. meɪ aɪ hæv jʊər aɪ diː ənd ˈkredɪt kɑːrd, pliːz?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Here you go. What time is breakfast served?
sentences.text_zh: 给您。早餐几点供应？
sentences.phonetic: /hɪər juː ɡəʊ. wɒt taɪm ɪz ˈbrekfəst sɜːrvd?/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Breakfast is served from 6:30 to 10:00 AM in the restaurant on the ground floor. Here are your room keys. The elevator is to your right.
sentences.text_zh: 早餐从早上6点30分到10点在一楼餐厅供应。这是您的房卡。电梯在您右手边。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈbrekfəst ɪz sɜːrvd frɒm sɪks ˈθɜːrti tuː ten əʊ klɒk eɪ em ɪn ðə ˈrestrɒnt ɒn ðə ɡraʊnd flɔːr. hɪər ɑːr jʊər ruːm kiːz. ði ˈelɪveɪtər ɪz tuː jʊər raɪt/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Thank you very much. Have a good evening!
sentences.text_zh: 非常感谢。祝您晚安！
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː ˈveri mʌtʃ. hæv ə ɡʊd ˈiːvnɪŋ!/

cultural_notes: US: first floor = ground floor. UK: ground floor ≠ first floor (first floor is one level up). Hotels typically require credit card for incidentals even if prepaid.
practice_tips: Learn hotel room types: standard, deluxe, suite. Practice ordinal numbers for floors: 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 15th.

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Hello, this is room 1508. I'd like to order room service, please.
sentences.text_zh: 你好，这是1508房间。我想点客房送餐服务。
sentences.phonetic: /həˈləʊ, ðɪs ɪz ruːm ˈfɪfˈtiːn əʊ eɪt. aɪd laɪk tuː ˈɔːrdər ruːm ˈsɜːrvɪs, pliːz/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Of course, sir. What would you like to have?
sentences.text_zh: 当然，先生。您想要什么呢？
sentences.phonetic: /ɒv kɔːrs, sɜːr. wɒt wʊd juː laɪk tuː hæv?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: I'll have the Caesar salad and a grilled chicken sandwich. Could I also get extra towels?
sentences.text_zh: 我要凯撒沙拉和一个烤鸡三明治。我还能要一些额外的毛巾吗？
sentences.phonetic: /aɪl hæv ðə ˈsiːzər ˈsæləd ənd ə ɡrɪld ˈtʃɪkɪn ˈsænwɪtʃ. kʊd aɪ ˈɔːlsəʊ ɡet ˈekstrə ˈtaʊəlz?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Certainly. Would you like anything to drink with that?
sentences.text_zh: 当然可以。您需要饮料吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈsɜːrtənli. wʊd juː laɪk ˈeniθɪŋ tuː drɪŋk wɪð ðæt?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Yes, a bottle of sparkling water, please. How long will it take?
sentences.text_zh: 是的，请来一瓶气泡水。需要多长时间？
sentences.phonetic: /jes, ə ˈbɒtl ɒv ˈspɑːrklɪŋ ˈwɔːtər, pliːz. haʊ lɒŋ wɪl ɪt teɪk?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: It should arrive in about 20 to 25 minutes. Is there anything else I can help you with?
sentences.text_zh: 大约20到25分钟送到。还有其他需要帮忙的吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɪt ʃʊd əˈraɪv ɪn əˈbaʊt ˈtwenti tuː ˈtwenti faɪv ˈmɪnɪts. ɪz ðeər ˈeniθɪŋ els aɪ kæn help juː wɪð?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: No, that's all. Thank you!
sentences.text_zh: 不用了，就这些。谢谢！
sentences.phonetic: /nəʊ, ðæts ɔːl. θæŋk juː!/

cultural_notes: Room service typically includes a delivery fee or service charge (15-20%). Tipping the delivery person $2-5 is customary in the US.
practice_tips: Practice 'I'd like' vs 'I'll have' - both work for ordering, but 'I'd like' is slightly more polite. Learn common food adjectives: grilled, fried, steamed, baked.

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Good morning. I'd like to check out, please. Room 1508.
sentences.text_zh: 早上好。我想退房。1508房间。
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˈmɔːrnɪŋ. aɪd laɪk tuː tʃek aʊt, pliːz. ruːm ˈfɪfˈtiːn əʊ eɪt/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Certainly, Mr. Smith. How was your stay?
sentences.text_zh: 当然，Smith先生。您住得怎么样？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈsɜːrtənli, ˈmɪstər smɪθ. haʊ wɒz jʊər steɪ?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: It was excellent, thank you. Could I see my bill before settling up?
sentences.text_zh: 非常好，谢谢。我能在结账前看一下账单吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɪt wɒz ˈeksələnt, θæŋk juː. kʊd aɪ siː maɪ bɪl bɪˈfɔːr ˈsetlɪŋ ʌp?/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Of course. Let me print that out for you. I see you had room service last night for $45, and there's a minibar charge of $12.
sentences.text_zh: 当然。让我给您打印出来。我看到您昨晚的客房送餐是45美元，还有12美元的迷你吧费用。
sentences.phonetic: /ɒv kɔːrs. let miː prɪnt ðæt aʊt fɔːr juː. aɪ siː juː hæd ruːm ˈsɜːrvɪs lɑːst naɪt fɔːr ˈfɔːrti faɪv ˈdɒlərz, ənd ðeərz ə ˈmɪnibɑːr tʃɑːrdʒ ɒv ˈtwelv ˈdɒlərz/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: That looks correct. Can I pay by credit card?
sentences.text_zh: 看起来是对的。我可以用信用卡支付吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ðæt lʊks kəˈrekt. kæn aɪ peɪ baɪ ˈkredɪt kɑːrd?/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Yes, I'll charge it to the card on file. Could you please sign here? Would you like a receipt?
sentences.text_zh: 是的，我会从您登记的卡中扣款。请在这里签名好吗？您需要收据吗？
sentences.phonetic: /jes, aɪl tʃɑːrdʒ ɪt tuː ðə kɑːrd ɒn faɪl. kʊd juː pliːz saɪn hɪər? wʊd juː laɪk ə rɪˈsiːt?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Yes, please. Also, could you call a taxi to the airport?
sentences.text_zh: 是的，请给我。还有，您能帮我叫一辆去机场的出租车吗？
sentences.phonetic: /jes, pliːz. ˈɔːlsəʊ, kʊd juː kɔːl ə ˈtæksi tuː ði ˈeəpɔːrt?/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Absolutely. It should be here in about 5 minutes. Thank you for staying with us!
sentences.text_zh: 当然。大约5分钟就到。感谢您入住我们酒店！
sentences.phonetic: /ˈæbsəluːtli. ɪt ʃʊd biː hɪər ɪn əˈbaʊt faɪv ˈmɪnɪts. θæŋk juː fɔːr ˈsteɪɪŋ wɪð ʌs!/

cultural_notes: Check-out time is typically 11 AM or 12 PM. Late check-out may incur extra charges. Always review your bill for unexpected charges (minibar, phone calls, parking).
practice_tips: Distinguish 'check in' (arrival) vs 'check out' (departure). Learn hotel charges vocabulary: room rate, tax, service charge, incidentals.

sentences.speaker: Person A
sentences.text_en: Good morning, everyone. Let me introduce myself. I'm Sarah Chen, the new Marketing Manager.
sentences.text_zh: 大家早上好。让我自我介绍一下。我是Sarah Chen，新任市场经理。
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˈmɔːrnɪŋ, ˈevriwʌn. let miː ˌɪntrəˈdjuːs maɪˈself. aɪm ˈsærə tʃen, ðə njuː ˈmɑːrkɪtɪŋ ˈmænɪdʒər/

sentences.speaker: Person A
sentences.text_en: I joined the company last month from our Singapore office. I'm excited to be working with this team.
sentences.text_zh: 我上个月从新加坡办公室加入公司。很高兴能与这个团队合作。
sentences.phonetic: /aɪ dʒɔɪnd ðə ˈkʌmpəni lɑːst mʌnθ frɒm ˈaʊər ˌsɪŋəˈpɔːr ˈɒfɪs. aɪm ɪkˈsaɪtɪd tuː biː ˈwɜːrkɪŋ wɪð ðɪs tiːm/

sentences.speaker: Person B
sentences.text_en: Welcome aboard, Sarah! I'm Tom Wilson, Sales Director. I've heard great things about your work in Singapore.
sentences.text_zh: 欢迎加入，Sarah！我是Tom Wilson，销售总监。我听说了很多关于您在新加坡工作的好评。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈwelkəm əˈbɔːrd, ˈsærə! aɪm tɒm ˈwɪlsən, seɪlz dəˈrektər. aɪv hɜːrd ɡreɪt θɪŋz əˈbaʊt jʊər wɜːrk ɪn ˌsɪŋəˈpɔːr/

sentences.speaker: Person A
sentences.text_en: Thank you, Tom. I'm looking forward to collaborating with sales on our upcoming campaigns.
sentences.text_zh: 谢谢，Tom。我期待与销售团队在即将到来的营销活动中合作。
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː, tɒm. aɪm ˈlʊkɪŋ ˈfɔːrwərd tuː kəˈlæbəreɪtɪŋ wɪð seɪlz ɒn ˈaʊər ˈʌpkʌmɪŋ kæmˈpeɪnz/

sentences.speaker: Person C
sentences.text_en: Hi Sarah, I'm Linda Park, Product Manager. If you need any support with product information, feel free to reach out.
sentences.text_zh: 你好Sarah，我是Linda Park，产品经理。如果您需要任何产品信息支持，请随时联系我。
sentences.phonetic: /haɪ ˈsærə, aɪm ˈlɪndə pɑːrk, ˈprɒdʌkt ˈmænɪdʒər. ɪf juː niːd ˈeni səˈpɔːrt wɪð ˈprɒdʌkt ˌɪnfərˈmeɪʃən, fiːl friː tuː riːtʃ aʊt/

sentences.speaker: Person A
sentences.text_en: That's very kind of you, Linda. I'm sure I'll take you up on that offer soon!
sentences.text_zh: 您真是太好了，Linda。我肯定很快就会接受您的帮助！
sentences.phonetic: /ðæts ˈveri kaɪnd ɒv juː, ˈlɪndə. aɪm ʃʊər aɪl teɪk juː ʌp ɒn ðæt ˈɒfər suːn/

cultural_notes: In Western business culture, introductions typically include name, title, and a brief background. It's common to offer help to new team members. Handshakes are standard (pre/post-pandemic considerations apply).
practice_tips: Master professional titles: Manager, Director, VP, C-level (CEO, CFO, CTO). Practice 'looking forward to' + gerund structure - very common in business English.

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: Alright, let's get started. The main agenda item today is our Q4 marketing strategy. Sarah, would you like to kick things off?
sentences.text_zh: 好的，我们开始吧。今天的主要议程是我们第四季度的市场策略。Sarah，您想先开始吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɔːlˈraɪt, lets ɡet ˈstɑːrtɪd. ðə meɪn əˈdʒendə ˈaɪtəm təˈdeɪ ɪz ˈaʊər kjuː fɔːr ˈmɑːrkɪtɪŋ ˈstrætədʒi. ˈsærə, wʊd juː laɪk tuː kɪk θɪŋz ɒf?/

sentences.speaker: Sarah
sentences.text_en: Absolutely. I've analyzed our current market position and identified three key areas we should focus on.
sentences.text_zh: 当然。我分析了我们当前的市场地位，并确定了我们应该关注的三个关键领域。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈæbsəluːtli. aɪv ˈænəlaɪzd ˈaʊər ˈkʌrənt ˈmɑːrkɪt pəˈzɪʃən ənd aɪˈdentɪfaɪd θriː kiː ˈeəriəz wiː ʃʊd ˈfəʊkəs ɒn/

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text_en: Before we dive in, could you clarify how this aligns with our sales targets?
sentences.text_zh: 在我们深入讨论之前，您能说明一下这如何与我们的销售目标保持一致吗？
sentences.phonetic: /bɪˈfɔːr wiː daɪv ɪn, kʊd juː ˈklærɪfaɪ haʊ ðɪs əˈlaɪnz wɪð ˈaʊər seɪlz ˈtɑːrɡɪts?/

sentences.speaker: Sarah
sentences.text_en: Good question. Each of these initiatives is designed to drive a 15% increase in qualified leads, which should translate to higher conversion rates.
sentences.text_zh: 好问题。这些举措中的每一项都旨在将合格线索增加15%，这应该能转化为更高的转化率。
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˈkwestʃən. iːtʃ ɒv ðiːz ɪˈnɪʃətɪvz ɪz dɪˈzaɪnd tuː draɪv ə ˈfɪfˈtiːn pərˈsent ɪnˈkriːs ɪn ˈkwɒlɪfaɪd liːdz, wɪtʃ ʃʊd trænsˈleɪt tuː ˈhaɪər kənˈvɜːrʒən reɪts/

sentences.speaker: Linda
sentences.text_en: That makes sense. However, I'm concerned about the timeline. Can we realistically achieve this by year-end?
sentences.text_zh: 这说得通。但是，我担心时间表。我们能在年底前实际完成这个目标吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ðæt meɪks sens. haʊˈevər, aɪm kənˈsɜːrnd əˈbaʊt ðə ˈtaɪmlaɪn. kæn wiː ˌrɪəˈlɪstɪkli əˈtʃiːv ðɪs baɪ jɪər end?/

sentences.speaker: Sarah
sentences.text_en: Fair point. If we prioritize digital channels and leverage automation tools, I believe it's achievable. We'd need full team buy-in, though.
sentences.text_zh: 说得对。如果我们优先考虑数字渠道并利用自动化工具，我相信是可以实现的。不过，我们需要整个团队的支持。
sentences.phonetic: /feər pɔɪnt. ɪf wiː praɪˈɒrɪtaɪz ˈdɪdʒɪtl ˈtʃænlz ənd ˈliːvərɪdʒ ˌɔːtəˈmeɪʃən tuːlz, aɪ bɪˈliːv ɪts əˈtʃiːvəbl. wiːd niːd fʊl tiːm baɪ ɪn, ðəʊ/

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: Excellent discussion. Let's table this for now and reconvene after reviewing Sarah's full proposal. Any objections?
sentences.text_zh: 讨论得很好。我们先暂时搁置这个，在审查Sarah的完整提案后再重新开会。有异议吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈeksələnt dɪˈskʌʃən. lets ˈteɪbl ðɪs fɔːr naʊ ənd ˌriːkənˈviːn ˈɑːftər rɪˈvjuːɪŋ ˈsærəz fʊl prəˈpəʊzl. ˈeni əbˈdʒekʃənz?/

cultural_notes: 'Table' means OPPOSITE things in US vs UK! US: postpone discussion. UK: bring up for discussion. In business meetings, disagreements should be expressed politely with phrases like 'I'm concerned...' or 'Could you clarify...'
practice_tips: Master business meeting phrases: 'kick off', 'dive in', 'table this', 'circle back', 'touch base'. Practice softening language: 'I believe', 'It seems', 'Perhaps we could'.

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: Alright, let's wrap up. To summarize, we've covered three main points today.
sentences.text_zh: 好的，我们总结一下。总结来说，我们今天讨论了三个要点。
sentences.phonetic: /ɔːlˈraɪt, lets ræp ʌp. tuː ˈsʌməraɪz, wiːv ˈkʌvərd θriː meɪn pɔɪnts təˈdeɪ/

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: First, we've agreed on the Q4 marketing strategy focusing on digital channels. Second, the budget allocation has been approved. Third, we've identified potential risks.
sentences.text_zh: 首先，我们同意了专注于数字渠道的第四季度市场策略。其次，预算分配已获批准。第三，我们已经识别了潜在风险。
sentences.phonetic: /fɜːrst, wiːv əˈɡriːd ɒn ðə kjuː fɔːr ˈmɑːrkɪtɪŋ ˈstrætədʒi ˈfəʊkəsɪŋ ɒn ˈdɪdʒɪtl ˈtʃænlz. ˈsekənd, ðə ˈbʌdʒɪt ˌæləˈkeɪʃən həz biːn əˈpruːvd. θɜːrd, wiːv aɪˈdentɪfaɪd pəˈtenʃəl rɪsks/

sentences.speaker: Sarah
sentences.text_en: Just to clarify, the timeline we discussed is still flexible pending final approvals from senior management, correct?
sentences.text_zh: 澄清一下，我们讨论的时间表仍然是灵活的，等待高级管理层的最终批准，对吗？
sentences.phonetic: /dʒʌst tuː ˈklærɪfaɪ, ðə ˈtaɪmlaɪn wiː dɪsˈkʌst ɪz stɪl ˈfleksəbl ˈpendɪŋ ˈfaɪnl əˈpruːvlz frɒm ˈsiːniər ˈmænɪdʒmənt, kəˈrekt?/

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: That's right. Moving on to action items: Sarah, you'll draft the detailed proposal by next Friday.
sentences.text_zh: 没错。接下来说行动项：Sarah，您要在下周五之前起草详细提案。
sentences.phonetic: /ðæts raɪt. ˈmuːvɪŋ ɒn tuː ˈækʃən ˈaɪtəmz: ˈsærə, juːl drɑːft ðə dɪˈteɪld prəˈpəʊzl baɪ nekst ˈfraɪdeɪ/

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: Tom, you're responsible for coordinating with the sales team on lead qualification criteria. Linda, please review the technical feasibility.
sentences.text_zh: Tom，您负责与销售团队协调线索资格标准。Linda，请审查技术可行性。
sentences.phonetic: /tɒm, jʊər rɪˈspɒnsəbl fɔːr kəʊˈɔːrdɪneɪtɪŋ wɪð ðə seɪlz tiːm ɒn liːd ˌkwɒlɪfɪˈkeɪʃən kraɪˈtɪəriə. ˈlɪndə, pliːz rɪˈvjuː ðə ˈteknɪkl ˌfiːzəˈbɪləti/

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text_en: Understood. I'll loop in the regional managers and have preliminary feedback by Wednesday.
sentences.text_zh: 明白了。我会让区域经理参与进来，并在周三之前给出初步反馈。
sentences.phonetic: /ˌʌndərˈstʊd. aɪl luːp ɪn ðə ˈriːdʒənl ˈmænɪdʒərz ənd hæv prɪˈlɪmɪnəri ˈfiːdbæk baɪ ˈwenzdeɪ/

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: Perfect. Let's schedule a follow-up meeting for two weeks from now. I'll send out calendar invites. Thanks everyone for your contributions today!
sentences.text_zh: 很好。我们安排两周后的后续会议。我会发送日历邀请。感谢大家今天的贡献！
sentences.phonetic: /ˈpɜːrfɪkt. lets ˈskedʒuːl ə ˈfɒləʊ ʌp ˈmiːtɪŋ fɔːr tuː wiːks frɒm naʊ. aɪl send aʊt ˈkælɪndər ɪnˈvaɪts. θæŋks ˈevriwʌn fɔːr jʊər ˌkɒntrɪˈbjuːʃənz təˈdeɪ/

cultural_notes: Effective meeting summaries include: key decisions, action items with owners and deadlines, and next steps. Always confirm understanding with phrases like 'Just to clarify...' or 'To confirm...'
practice_tips: Master summary phrases: 'To sum up', 'In conclusion', 'To recap'. Learn action item language: 'You're responsible for', 'Please ensure', 'I'll take care of', 'Let's circle back on'.
